# Your Task

Create an Event Registration System for an event management company.

The system should consists of 2 portions:

* One is used by employees to create new events and to view reports.  
* Another one is used by the general public to register for the events.


An event should at least have the following attributes:

* Name of the event  
* Date & time of the event  
* Address \- location of the event  
* Registration deadline  
* Handler \- employee who is in charge of the event  
* Capacity \- maximum number of registration allowed

An event is considered open for registration when:

* Its registration deadline has not been reached.  
* Its capacity has not been reached. 

# Requirements

1. You should use the following tech stacks to implement the system:  
* General:  
  * Typescript  
  * HTTP client: axios  
* Backend API:  
  * Express.js  
  * Database: MySQL  
  * ORM: Sequelize  
  * Logger: winston  
  * Test runner: jest  
* Frontend:  
  * React  
  * React-router  
  * UI: any react UI library, e.g. Material UI, Bootstrap, shadcn, Chakra UI, etc. 


2. Use the async/await syntax instead of Promise chaining.  
     
3. Authentication and authorisation is not required.

4. Your API will be subjected to automated test tools, please adhere closely to the given specifications (according to the User Stories section).  
     
5. Rule for API http status:  
   * Successful: 200  
   * Validation error: 421  
   * Expected client error: 400  
   * Unexpected / server error: 500

   

6. Your system should have proper data validation and apply proper security practices.

7. Backend unit tests must be included and should have at least 50% coverage.  
     
8. UI does not need to match exactly with the sample given, just needs rough resemblance.  
     
9. API should be running on port 8000\. Front-end should be running on port 8001\.

10. Your code must be hosted on GitHub, or any other similar publicly accessible code repository.  
      
11. Include a README.md that contains:  
    * Instructions for setting up the database.  
    * Instructions for seeding the initial data for data that is not manageable through the system.  
    * Instructions for running the system locally.  
    * Instructions for running unit tests locally.

12. Your codebase and code repository, including all files, components and descriptions, must not contain the keyword “Ufinity”.  
      
13. Send us the URL of the code repository containing the completed assignment. 

# 

# 

# User Stories

1. ## Allow employees to view all events.

![][image1]

* Events result must be paginated.  
* Events can be searched by partial words of name, address or handler.  
* Events can be filtered by status, open=true means to filter events that are still open for registration only.

**API Request**  
**Method:** GET  
**Endpoint:** /api/admin/events  
**Params:**  
{  
 page: number;  
 search?: string;  
 open?: true;  
}

**API Response**  
{  
 total: number;  
 events: {  
   uuid: string;  
   createdAt: string;  
   name: string;  
   dateTime: string;  
   address: string;  
   deadline: string;  
   capacity: number;  
   registrationCount: number;  
   handler: {  
     uuid: string;  
     name: string;  
   };  
 }\[\];  
}

## 

2. ## Allow employees to add an event.

![][image2]

* The event name must be unique.  
* Postal code must be validated against OneMap API. (see OneMap API section below)  
* A handler (employee in charge) is allowed to handle maximum 1 open event at any point of time.  
* Apply other validations which you see fits.  
* Error should be clearly shown to employees.


**Expected API Request**  
**Method:** POST  
**Endpoint:** /api/admin/events  
**Body**:  
{  
 name: string;  
 dateTime: string;  
 postalCode: string;  
 deadline: string;  
 capacity: number;  
 handlerUuid: string;  
}

**API Response**  
nil

## 

3. ## Allow the general public to view all open events.

![][image3]  
![][image4]

* Open events are to be displayed in a select dropdown.  
* Once an event is selected, display its information below.

**API Request**  
**Method:** GET  
**Endpoint:** /api/public/events

**API Response**  
{  
 uuid: string;  
 name: string;  
 dateTime: string;  
 address: string;  
}\[\]

## 

4. ## Allow the general public to register for an event.

![][image5]

* To register, users need to select an event and enter their email address.  
* Once registration is accepted, show a generic thank you screen that includes the registration number.  
* Registration number should be a 5 digits running number that is unique to the event, e.g. 00001  
* Error should be clearly shown to users.

**API Request**  
**Method:** POST  
**Endpoint:** /api/public/register  
**Body:**  
{  
 eventUuid: string;  
 emailAddress: string;  
}

**API Response**  
{  
 registrationNo: string;  
}

5. ## Allow employees to view the registration trend of an event.

![][image6]

* By clicking the ‘Click Trend’ button in the event listing table, a table that contains the registration trend of the event will show.  
* The table should consist of all dates from event creation date till registration deadline. For each date, show the total registration and new registration on the date.

**API Request**  
**Method:** POST  
**Endpoint:** /api/admin/events/:uuid/trend

**API Response**  
{  
 date: string;  
 registrationCount: number;  
 newRegistrationCount: number;  
}\[\]

# OneMap API

**Register a free account**  
[https://www.onemap.gov.sg/apidocs/register](https://www.onemap.gov.sg/apidocs/register)

**API for getting access token**  
[https://www.onemap.gov.sg/apidocs/authentication](https://www.onemap.gov.sg/apidocs/authentication)

**API for searching address by postal code**  
[https://www.onemap.gov.sg/apidocs/search](https://www.onemap.gov.sg/apidocs/search)
